ogTitle: Haberfield vs Hacks Ferry: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Haberfield and Hacks Ferry.
heading: Haberfield vs Hacks Ferry — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Haberfield, New South Wales and Hacks Ferry, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hacks Ferry has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Haberfield (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Haberfield or Hacks Ferry safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Haberfield has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Hacks Ferry scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Haberfield scores 65/100 for bushfire risk. Hacks Ferry scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Haberfield or Hacks Ferry?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hacks Ferry has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
